title: The echo state approach to analysing and training recurrent neural networks
authors: Jaeger H.
landmark: ESN founding paper — introduced echo state networks and the echo state property

title: Real-time computing without stable states: a new framework for neural computation based on perturbations
authors: Maass W. / Natschlaeger T. / Markram H.
landmark: LSM founding paper — introduced liquid state machines with spiking neurons

title: Harnessing nonlinearity: predicting chaotic systems and saving energy in wireless communication
authors: Jaeger H. / Haas H.
landmark: First high-impact application — chaotic time-series prediction in Science journal; brought RC to wider community

title: Edge of chaos and prediction of computational performance for neural microcircuit models
authors: Legenstein R. / Maass W.
landmark: Introduced edge-of-chaos principle — theoretical foundation for reservoir dynamics regime

title: An experimental unification of reservoir computing methods
authors: Verstraeten D. / Schrauwen B. / D'Haene M. / Stroobandt D.
landmark: Unified ESN and LSM under the single term Reservoir Computing — conceptual unification of the field

title: Reservoir computing approaches to recurrent neural network training
authors: Lukosevicius M. / Jaeger H.
landmark: Definitive training survey — most-cited RC methods paper; reference for practitioners

title: Information processing capacity of dynamical systems
authors: Dambre J. / Verstraeten D. / Schrauwen B. / Massar S.
landmark: Capacity theorem — formal framework for measuring RC computational capacity; theoretical landmark

title: Information processing using a single dynamical node as complex system
authors: Appeltant L. / Soriano M.C. / Van der Sande G. et al.
landmark: Single-node delay RC — opened hardware implementation with minimal physical complexity; key for photonics

title: Minimum complexity echo state network
authors: Rodan A. / Tino P.
landmark: Showed simple ring topology works — challenged assumption that complex random reservoirs are necessary

title: A practical guide to applying echo state networks
authors: Lukosevicius M.
venue: Neural Networks: Tricks of the Trade (Springer)
landmark: Standard practitioner reference — hyperparameter tuning guide used by the entire community

title: Optoelectronic reservoir computing
authors: Paquot Y. / Duport F. / Smerieri A. et al.
landmark: First optoelectronic RC implementation — landmark for physical/photonic RC community

title: Photonic information processing beyond Turing: an optoelectronic implementation of reservoir computing
authors: Larger L. / Soriano M.C. / Brunner D. et al.
landmark: Parallel photonic RC — demonstrated real-time hardware photonic reservoir with delay lines

title: Parallel photonic information processing at gigabyte per second data rates using transient states
authors: Brunner D. / Soriano M.C. / Mirasso C.R. / Fischer I.
landmark: GHz-speed photonic RC — pushed photonic RC to real-world speeds; key scaling milestone

title: Separation of variables and universality in reservoir computing
authors: Grigoryeva L. / Ortega J.P.
landmark: Universality result — proved RC with fading memory can approximate any causal time-invariant filter

title: Deep echo state network (DeepESN): a brief survey
authors: Gallicchio C. / Micheli A.
landmark: Deep RC — introduced hierarchical stacking of reservoirs; opened deep learning perspective in RC

title: Model-free prediction of large spatiotemporally chaotic systems from data: a reservoir computing approach
authors: Pathak J. / Hunt B. / Girvan M. / Lu Z. / Ott E.
landmark: RC predicts chaos — demonstrated RC outperforming LSTM on chaotic Kuramoto-Sivashinsky; PRL landmark

title: Reservoir computing universality with stochastic inputs
authors: Grigoryeva L. / Ortega J.P.
venue: IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks and Learning Systems
landmark: Stochastic universality — extended universality proof to stochastic input settings

title: Recent advances in physical reservoir computing: a review
authors: Tanaka G. / Yamane T. / Heroux J.B. et al.
landmark: Physical RC review — comprehensive survey that defined the physical RC subfield and taxonomy

title: Quantum reservoir processing
authors: Fujii K. / Nakajima K.
landmark: Quantum RC founding paper — showed quantum systems can serve as reservoirs with exponential state space

title: Hybrid forecasting of chaotic processes: using machine learning in conjunction with a knowledge-based model
authors: Pathak J. / Wikner A. / Fussell R. et al.
landmark: Hybrid RC + physics-based model — pioneered combining domain knowledge with RC for forecasting

title: Next generation reservoir computing
authors: Gauthier D.J. / Bollt E. / Griffith A. / Barbosa W.A.S.
landmark: Next-gen RC — replaced random reservoir with nonlinear vector autoregression; reduced parameters dramatically

title: Recent advances in physical reservoir computing: a review
authors: Nakajima K.
landmark: Physical RC perspectives — roadmap paper for the physical RC community by a leading figure

title: Emerging opportunities and challenges for the future of reservoir computing
authors: Yan M. / Huang C. / Bienstman P. et al.
landmark: Future RC roadmap — Nature Communications perspective summarizing open problems and directions

title: Task-adaptive physical reservoir computing
authors: Lee O. / Wei T. / Stenning K.D. et al.
landmark: Adaptive physical RC — demonstrated task-switchable magnetic reservoir; milestone for physical adaptability
